class.name: MultiNodeRaycast
class.brief_description: Per-instance raycasting for ground detection, line of sight, and surface probing.
class.description: MultiNodeRaycast casts a ray from each active instance every physics frame. Results are stored per-instance and queryable via is_instance_colliding(), get_instance_hit_point(), etc. Rays automatically exclude sibling MultiNodeCollider bodies to avoid self-hits. Use cases include ground detection for vehicles, line-of-sight checks for AI, terrain following, and wall detection.

members.member: The direction each ray is cast, relative to the instance. Default is straight down. When use_instance_basis is enabled, this direction rotates with the instance. The vector is normalized automatically.
How far each ray extends from the instance origin, in meters. Longer rays are more expensive. Set to the minimum distance needed for your use case.
The physics layer bitmask that rays detect. Only bodies on matching layers are hit.
When enabled, rays can hit the inside of collision shapes. Useful when instances start inside geometry and need to detect the enclosing surface.

method.name: is_instance_colliding
method.description: Check whether the ray for a specific instance hit something last physics frame.

method.name: get_instance_hit_point
method.description: Get the world-space position where the ray hit. Returns the ray endpoint if nothing was hit.

method.name: get_instance_hit_normal
method.description: Get the surface normal at the hit point. Returns zero vector if nothing was hit.

method.name: get_instance_hit_distance
method.description: Get the distance from the ray origin to the hit point. Returns the full cast_length if nothing was hit.

method.name: get_instance_hit_rid
method.description: Get the RID of the collider that was hit. Can be passed to a sibling MultiNodeCollider's get_instance_from_body() to identify which instance was hit.
